job_description:
Sr. B2B Content Specialist

Company Overview 2Base Technologies is a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, serving mid-market and enterprise clients in the US and UK. Our expertise spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

Role Purpose This senior writing role owns the written voice of the company end-to-end. It is not a content management or social media role. The role operationalises and enforces the brand's voice across all written output, ensuring technical credibility, commercial relevance, and a clear point of view.

Key Responsibilities - Own all long-form content (pillar pages, blogs, playbooks, decision frameworks, use-case libraries) targeting senior decision-makers in the US and UK. - Write content in the voice of senior leadership for professional publishing. - Audit and enforce the 2Base content voice across all published materials. - Create clear content briefs executable by junior team members. - Act as the content quality gatekeeper for all published work. - Use AI tools as a productivity layer, not a substitute for original thinking.

What You Won't Own - SEO keyword selection (managed by the SEO Specialist). - Social media scheduling (managed by a separate team member). - Visual design (managed by the Visual Designer). - Distribution strategy (managed by the Marketing & Growth Lead). - ICP definition (you write for the ICP, but do not define it).

Requirements - 3-5 years of B2B content writing experience (strong candidates have 5-6 years). - Proven ability to create content that ranks on Google. - Experience writing in voices other than your own (executives, founders, senior professionals). - Working-level SEO knowledge (understands search intent). - Experience with AI writing tools, balancing their value and limitations. - Demonstrable content contributions to measurable outcomes (traffic, leads, engagement). - Background in technology, SaaS, or professional services preferred.

Writing Ability - Writes with a clear point of view, not neutral summaries. - Explains complex technical concepts in plain language without losing credibility. - Writes concisely, avoiding filler and unnecessary qualification. - Adapts to different voices authentically.

Mindset - Treats content as a commercial tool, not a creative exercise. - Comfortable receiving and acting on feedback without taking it personally. - Curious about AI, technology, and business operations. - Fast-paced: delivers drafts quickly without delays.

What We Are Not Looking For - Portfolios of generic "Top 10 Tips" articles or trend summaries. - Writers who cannot explain their content's performance or impact. - Candidates without experience writing for technical or senior business audiences. - Writers who only produce self-directed content and lack brief adherence. - Candidates who rely on AI tools as the primary author with minimal editing.

Benefits - A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, share your resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Senior B2B Content Specialist needed to own and enforce 2Base Technologies' written voice across technical and business audiences, ensuring high-quality, commercially relevant content.

job_description:
Organic Growth & SEO Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the centre of it. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process. The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

This is a strategic and technical role. It is not an SEO support role, a blogging role with keyword research added on, or a task-execution position within a larger team.

What You Will Own

  • The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline
  • Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences
  • Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking
  • On-page SEO across all published pages
  • Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data
  • Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification
  • The programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages
  • SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing
  • Link building target approval. The outreach team identifies candidates, and you approve or reject based on defined quality standards
  • SEO performance reporting, including organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, and Search Console health

What You Will Not Own

  • Writing content: you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages
  • Link building outreach: a dedicated team member executes this, you set the standard
  • Paid search: that is a separate role
  • Social media distribution: separate channel, separate owner
  • Content calendar scheduling: the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities

Requirements

Experience - Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role, with strong candidates typically at the 4 to 6 year mark - Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content - Experience managing technical SEO independently - Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture - Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool - Experience working across US and/or UK markets - Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO, including structuring content for AI-powered search surfaces - Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without clarification

Mindset - Thinks in systems, not tasks - Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments - Analytically honest - Collaborative but not passive

What We Are Not Looking For

  • SEO practitioners whose entire experience is informational content with no connection to commercial intent or pipeline
  • Anyone who treats Search Console as a monthly reporting task
  • Candidates who cannot explain the difference between high-intent and informational keyword targeting
  • Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on a tool telling them what to do next
  • Candidates with no exposure to US or UK search behaviour who treat all markets as equivalent

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Strategic Organic Growth & SEO Specialist building scalable systems for US/UK markets. Owns technical SEO, programmatic architecture, and high-intent content strategy.

job_description:
Sr. Visual Designer 2Base Technologies

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from scratch, and this role makes it look like a company worth taking seriously. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

Before a CTO reads a word of our content, they see the design. Before they trust the argument, they have already made a judgment about whether this company looks credible. This role owns that first impression, and then owns every asset that follows.

The visual function at 2Base is being built from the ground up. There is no brand system to inherit, no template library to use, and no consistent visual identity to maintain. What exists is a clear strategic direction, a defined audience of senior technology and operations leaders, and a marketing team building the content engine around it. Your job is to give that engine a visual identity it deserves.

The first 30 days of this role have one priority: build the brand system. Everything else, including LinkedIn carousels, ad creatives, landing pages, and presentation templates, is built from that foundation. A designer who wants to jump straight into production before the system exists is not the right fit.

This is not a creative agency role. The audience is sceptical, senior, and technically literate. Design that looks clever but reads as unclear, or feels consumer-facing rather than professional, will not work with this ICP. Restraint, precision, and consistency matter more than visual novelty.

What You Will Own

  • The 2Base visual brand system, including colour palette, typography, spacing, layout rules, and the documentation that makes it usable by the whole team
  • All marketing and content visual assets, including LinkedIn carousels, single-image posts, social graphics, infographics, and diagrams
  • Paid advertising creatives, including Google Display and LinkedIn Ads, designed to be tested and iterated
  • Landing page visual design, including layout, components, and conversion-focused structure, handed to the development team for build
  • Email visual templates, presentation templates, and sales deck templates
  • A template library that enables other team members to produce on-brand assets independently
  • Design consistency auditing, including periodic review of all published assets to ensure alignment with the brand system
  • Secondary responsibility: light video editing and motion for social reels, YouTube, and website use, supporting other team members

What You Will Not Own

  • What an asset communicates. The Content and Thought Leadership Specialist owns the message, argument, and information hierarchy. You may suggest improvements in structure, but content decisions remain with them
  • Copywriting. The Content Specialist writes the words; you structure them visually
  • Website development. You design the pages; the development team builds them
  • Content strategy. You execute what is briefed, not decide what gets created
  • Brand positioning. The Marketing Operating System defines how 2Base communicates; you interpret it visually

Requirements

Experience - Minimum 3 to 5 years in a B2B design role, preferably in technology, SaaS, or professional services - Experience building a brand system or significantly evolving one, not just producing isolated assets - Experience designing for LinkedIn, including carousel formats, document posts, and single-image feed content - Experience creating paid ad creatives that have been executed, tested, and iterated - Experience designing landing pages with an understanding of conversion principles - Comfort with basic video editing for social and web content, including reels, short-form YouTube, and simple website assets - Proficiency in Figma (non-negotiable) and Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, After Effects)

Design Thinking - Portfolio demonstrates restraint through clean, structured, and purposeful design - Ability to explain the reasoning behind key design decisions - Designs with a specific audience in mind, not for general appeal - Strong understanding and consistent application of typography as a functional tool - Ability to produce work that appears credible to a senior B2B audience at first glance

Mindset - Able to work effectively from structured briefs - Works at speed without compromising quality - Treats design as a communication tool rather than purely creative expression - Open to feedback and able to give it constructively

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios dominated by consumer brands, lifestyle work, event design, or campaigns with no B2B relevance
  • Designers who can describe visuals but cannot justify their decisions
  • Candidates without experience building a brand system
  • Designers who overcomplicate visuals with excessive fonts, colours, styles, or effects
  • Candidates who require complete creative freedom and struggle with structured briefs

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Lead the creation of 2Base Technologies' visual brand system and marketing assets for senior B2B audiences. Requires 3-5 years of B2B design experience and expertise in Figma and Adobe tools.

job_description:

Requirements - Basic understanding of Linux operating systems. - Knowledge of networking concepts (IP, DNS, HTTP, firewalls). - Familiarity with system troubleshooting and command-line usage. - Basic understanding of computer systems and infrastructure concepts. - Interest in cloud computing, infrastructure automation, and security. - Beginner-level scripting knowledge (Bash/Shell/Python). - Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset. - Willingness to learn and work in infrastructure and security domains. - Good communication and teamwork skills.

job_description:
Sr. DevSecOps Engineer

We are seeking a Senior DevSecOps Engineer to lead our cybersecurity, infrastructure automation, and DevSecOps practices as we transition to a secure, scalable, and modern engineering environment. This role will strengthen 2Base’s security posture, modernize pipelines, and foster a secure-by-design culture. You will collaborate with engineering, product, and leadership teams to ensure systems, applications, and cloud environments are secure, reliable, and future-ready.

Required Skills & Experience - 4–8 years in DevOps, Cloud, or Infrastructure engineering with strong security exposure. - Hands-on CI/CD tools: Jenkins, GitLab CI, GitHub Actions. - Expertise in AWS, Azure, or GCP with secure architecture focus. - Proficiency in Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform, Ansible). - Experience securing Docker and Kubernetes environments. - Strong networking knowledge: LAN/WAN, firewalls, VPN, load balancers. - Familiarity with security frameworks (OWASP, NIST, CIS Benchmarks). - Monitoring/logging tools: Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, CloudWatch, SIEM. - Scripting skills: Python, Bash, Shell. - Excellent troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills. - Strong communication and collaboration abilities to influence engineering teams.

Benefits - Growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact: Email your resume to careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Lead cybersecurity and infrastructure automation, ensuring secure cloud environments and DevSecOps practices across AWS/Azure/GCP. Collaborate with teams to enforce security and compliance.

job_description:
QA Engineer

We are seeking a detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of hands-on experience in software testing to join our QA team. In this role, you will ensure the quality and reliability of web and mobile applications by participating in test planning, test case creation, test execution, and defect tracking. You will collaborate with developers, project teams, and senior QA engineers to understand requirements, identify risks, and validate application functionality across scenarios. The role demands a strong testing mindset, attention to detail, and the ability to identify edge cases and potential product issues.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Review BRD, SRS, user stories, and functional specifications to understand system requirements.
  • Translate requirements into structured test scenarios and documented test cases for comprehensive coverage.
  • Perform functional, regression, and exploratory testing for web and mobile applications across browsers, devices, and environments.
  • Identify edge cases, alternate flows, and boundary conditions to validate system behavior beyond standard use cases.
  • Log, document, and track defects with clear reproduction steps, evidence, and severity classification using defect management tools.
  • Validate bug fixes and perform regression testing to ensure stability during releases.
  • Participate in release testing cycles and sprint-level validation to confirm feature readiness.
  • Maintain test documentation, execution reports, and QA artifacts for traceability and quality tracking.
  • Support test summaries, progress updates, and QA reports during development and release cycles.
  • Collaborate with business analysts, developers, project managers, and senior QA engineers to clarify requirements and resolve issues.
  • Participate in Agile/Scrum ceremonies (sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews).
  • Perform basic API validation and data verification to ensure correct system behavior at the integration level.
  • Take ownership of assigned modules or features to ensure testing completeness and release readiness.
  • Contribute to improving test coverage, testing approaches, and QA processes within the team.
  • Assist in testing AI-powered or data-driven features by validating output accuracy, response consistency, and system behavior across inputs.
  • Continuously learn automation fundamentals, structured testing techniques, and emerging QA tools, including AI testing practices.

Required Skills & Mindset:

  • Strong attention to detail and structured thinking.
  • Willingness to deeply understand business logic before testing.
  • Ability to identify edge cases and risks beyond happy paths.
  • Analytical mindset to question unexpected system behavior.
  • Clear written and verbal communication for collaboration.
  • Strong documentation skills for defect reporting and test case writing.
  • Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.
  • Solid understanding of functional, regression, integration, and exploratory testing.
  • Experience with test case design techniques.
  • Familiarity with bug tracking and test management tools.
  • Basic exposure to API testing (Postman, REST Assured).
  • Understanding of Agile/Scrum methodologies and QA’s role in sprint cycles.
  • Familiarity with AI-assisted testing.

Good to Have:

  • Basic awareness of performance and security testing.
  • Interest in learning automation frameworks (Selenium, Playwright, Cypress).
  • Scripting knowledge in Python, JavaScript, or Java.
  • Exposure to AI-enabled applications (chatbots, recommendation engines, generative AI) and testing aspects like prompt variations and response accuracy.
  • Awareness of modern QA practices, including continuous testing in software delivery pipelines.
  • Ability to leverage AI-assisted tools for test case generation, test data preparation, and productivity improvements.
  • Curiosity about test data management and synthetic/structured test data generation.
  • Experience testing mobile applications or cross-platform systems, or openness to learning mobile testing frameworks (Appium).
  • Strong inclination toward risk-based testing and prioritization.
  • Commitment to continuous learning and staying updated with QA tools, methodologies, and industry practices.
  • ISTQB certification or equivalent testing certification is an added advantage.

Experience & Education:

  • 1–3 years of experience in QA testing.
  • B.Tech in IT, CS, or MCA (Graduates).

Contact: Share your updated resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: A detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of experience in manual and automation testing for web/mobile applications, collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure product quality and validate AI-driven features.
